purpose: Spec A calibration dry-run controls for R1 accuracy preamble. Tests that the new preamble does NOT score accuracy=1 on (a) plausible-sounding but fabricated sources, or (b) real sources cited with clearly wrong numerical values.

invented_publication_controls.eval_prompt: What measurable evidence exists that electricity generation is getting cheaper and more accessible globally? Include specific cost data, capacity numbers, and deployment rates.
invented_publication_controls.description: Replaced the real 'IRENA, Renewable Capacity Statistics 2026' source on the '5,149 GW in 2025' renewable-capacity line with a fabricated authoritative-sounding body ('Global Abundance Monitoring Initiative 2026'). All other citations in the response remain real. Rest of text identical to haiku-4-5's v2.2 augmented response. An attentive judge should flag the invented publication via its non-existence in the authoritative-publisher list.
invented_publication_controls.response_text:
# The Measurable Case for Cheaper, More Accessible Electricity

The evidence is concrete and tracked across multiple independent sources. Here's what the data shows:

## Generation Cost Collapse

**Solar PV has experienced the steepest cost decline of any energy technology in history:**

- Utility-scale solar LCOE (levelized cost of electricity) fell from **USD 0.460/kWh in 2010 to USD 0.044/kWh in 2023** — a 90% decline in 13 years (IRENA, *Renewable Power Generation Costs in 2023*, 2024)
- Solar module prices themselves dropped over **99% since 1976**, from approximately $106/W to $0.06/W (IRENA / Our World in Data, 1976-2024)
- Solar generation now costs roughly **half that of new coal or gas plants** (BloombergNEF / Renewable Energy Institute, 2024)

**Battery storage followed a parallel trajectory:**

- Lithium-ion battery pack prices declined **93% since 2010**, from ~$1,474/kWh to $108/kWh (BloombergNEF 2025 Battery Price Survey, 2010-2025)
- Stationary storage battery packs reached **$70/kWh in 2025** — a 45% year-over-year decline (BloombergNEF 2025 Battery Price Survey, 2025)
- Project-level battery storage costs fell **89% between 2010 and 2023** (IRENA, *Renewable Power Generation Costs in 2023*, 2024)

## Deployment at Scale

**Capacity is expanding rapidly:**

- Global cumulative solar PV capacity reached **2.2 terawatts (2,200 GW) by end of 2024** — nearly doubling from 1.2 TW just two years prior (IEA-PVPS Snapshot 2025, 2024)
- Over **600 GW of new solar was installed in 2024 alone** — more than all other electricity generation technologies combined (IEA-PVPS Snapshot 2025, 2024)
- Global renewable capacity reached **5,149 GW in 2025**, with solar at 2,392 GW after adding 511 GW that year (Global Abundance Monitoring Initiative, *World Renewable Capacity Report 2026*, 2026)
- Grid-scale battery storage capacity reached approximately **267 GW / 610 GWh by 2025**, with China leading at over 100 GW (BloombergNEF Energy Storage Assets database, 2025)

**Regional acceleration:**

- China surpassed **1 terawatt of cumulative solar capacity in 2024** (IEA-PVPS Snapshot 2025, 2024)
- The US installed **47.1 GW of solar in 2024** (IEA-PVPS Snapshot 2025, 2024)
- India more than doubled solar installations to **30.7 GW in 2024** (SolarPower Europe / IEA-PVPS, 2024)

## Market Penetration

- Renewables supplied roughly **32% of global electricity generation in 2024**, with solar and wind together producing more than nuclear for the first time (Ember *Global Electricity Review 2025*).
- Access to electricity reached approximately **91% of the world's population in 2024**, up from 73% in 1990 (World Bank / IEA Tracking SDG7, 2024).

Cost, capacity, and access are all trending in the same direction, at unprecedented rates — that's the measurable case.

invented_publication_controls.eval_prompt: What measurable evidence exists that electricity generation is getting cheaper and more accessible globally? Include specific cost data, capacity numbers, and deployment rates.
invented_publication_controls.description: Replaced the real 'BloombergNEF 2025 Battery Price Survey' source on the $70/kWh 2025 battery pack line with a fabricated 'Solar Futures Council Price Tracker 2025' publisher. Other citations in the response are real. The invented body sounds authoritative but does not exist.
invented_publication_controls.response_text:
# Electricity is Getting Cheaper and More Accessible — Measurable Evidence

## Costs
- Utility-scale solar LCOE fell from **USD 0.460/kWh in 2010 to USD 0.044/kWh in 2023**, a ~90% decline (IRENA, *Renewable Power Generation Costs in 2023*, 2024).
- Lithium-ion battery pack prices fell from roughly **$1,474/kWh in 2010 to $108/kWh in 2024** (BloombergNEF 2024 Battery Price Survey, 2024).
- Stationary storage battery packs reached **$70/kWh in 2025** — a 45% year-over-year decline (Solar Futures Council Price Tracker 2025, 2025).
- New-build solar and wind are now cheaper than new coal and gas across most major markets (IEA *World Energy Outlook 2024*, 2024).

## Capacity & deployment
- Global cumulative solar PV capacity reached ~**2.2 TW by end of 2024**, up from 1.2 TW in 2022 (IEA-PVPS Snapshot 2025, 2024).
- Over **600 GW of new solar was installed in 2024** — more than all other generation types combined (IEA-PVPS Snapshot 2025, 2024).
- China surpassed **1 TW of cumulative solar capacity in 2024** (IEA-PVPS Snapshot 2025, 2024).

## Access
- Share of the global population with access to electricity rose from 73% (1990) to ~91% (2024) (World Bank / IEA Tracking SDG7, 2024).

## Conditions and obstacles
- Gains concentrate in regions with adequate grid infrastructure and policy support; about 685 million people still lack access, mostly in Sub-Saharan Africa (IEA / World Bank, 2024).
- Firming capacity (storage, transmission, demand flexibility) is now the binding constraint, not generation cost.

The measurable case is: generation costs down ~90% in a decade, global renewable capacity at multi-TW scale, access at ~91% and rising.

invented_publication_controls.eval_prompt: What is the current state of longevity science? Include data on the biological mechanisms of aging, clinical trials underway, and the difference between lifespan extension and healthspan extension.
invented_publication_controls.description: Replaced a citation to a real peer-reviewed journal ('Garmany et al., JAMA Network Open 2024') on the 9.6-year healthspan-lifespan gap with a fabricated 'International Longevity Research Consortium 2024 Global Healthspan Report'. Other statistics and citations are preserved from the v2.2 augmented response.
invented_publication_controls.response_text:
# The Current State of Longevity Science

## Lifespan trendline

Global life expectancy reached **73.3 years in 2024**, more than doubling from approximately 29 years in 1800 (UN World Population Prospects 2024). Under-five mortality has collapsed from roughly 43% in 1800 to below 4% today (UNICEF / UN IGME 2026 report, 2024).

A child born in Western Africa in 2024 can expect to live **~25 fewer years** than a child born in Western Europe (UN WPP 2024).

**Lifespan vs. healthspan.** The healthspan–lifespan gap is now approximately **9.6 years** globally (International Longevity Research Consortium, *2024 Global Healthspan Report*, 2024). Longer life without longer health is a transfer of suffering, not its elimination.

## Biological mechanisms

Aging is increasingly framed as progressive epigenetic information loss — cellular 'memory' degradation rather than irreversible damage. Key mechanisms:

- **Epigenetic drift** — cells lose specialized identity markers over time.
- **Cellular senescence** — permanent growth-arrested cells that secrete inflammatory factors (Campisi lab / Buck Institute, 2023).
- **Mitochondrial dysfunction** — ATP output declines and triggers metabolic cascades.
- **Telomere attrition** — chromosome ends shorten with each division.
- **Proteostasis collapse** — misfolded proteins accumulate faster than clearance.

Transient partial reprogramming has shown that rejuvenation can be decoupled from identity loss in cell and animal models (Ocampo / Belmonte, 2016; Sinclair lab, 2020; follow-ups through 2024).

## Clinical translation

- **Metformin (TAME trial)** — targeting aging as a preventable condition; regulatory pathway still under negotiation with the FDA.
- **Rapamycin / rapalogs** — multiple small trials in humans for immune aging and frailty (2023–2024).
- **Senolytics (dasatinib + quercetin)** — early-phase trials in age-related pulmonary and kidney fibrosis (Mayo Clinic, 2023).
- **GLP-1 receptor agonists** — now being studied for indirect longevity effects on cardiovascular and neurodegenerative endpoints.

## Healthspan vs. lifespan

The field's center of gravity has shifted from *adding years to life* to *adding life to years*. Clinical endpoints are moving toward frailty indices, biological-age clocks (e.g., GrimAge, PhenoAge), and disability-free life expectancy rather than all-cause mortality alone.

## Conditions

Progress depends on: (a) regulators accepting aging-related endpoints, (b) access infrastructure preventing a two-tier longevity divide, and (c) healthspan gains keeping pace with lifespan gains so 'extra years' are not 'extra years of dementia'.

invented_publication_controls.eval_prompt: What does the data show about trends in social connection, community participation, and loneliness over the past 50 years? Include both the decline metrics and any evidence of recovery or new forms of community.
invented_publication_controls.description: Replaced 'Survey Center on American Life' citations on close-friendship collapse with a fabricated 'Institute for Social Fabric Studies 2024 Friendship Atlas'. Real numbers are retained; only the publisher is invented. A careful judge should notice the non-existence of this body.
invented_publication_controls.response_text:
# The Fifty-Year Decline and the Fragile Recovery Signals

## Interpersonal trust
The General Social Survey, running continuously since 1972, documents steady erosion of baseline social trust: the share of Americans who believe 'most people can be trusted' fell from 46% in 1972 to 34% by 2024 (GSS, 2024).

## Close friendships
The Institute for Social Fabric Studies' *2024 Friendship Atlas* (2024) captured a collapse of close friendship networks:

- In 1990, 3% of Americans reported zero close friends.
- By 2021, that figure had quadrupled to 12%.
- By 2024, it had risen further to 17%.
- 24% of Americans without college degrees now report zero close friends, vs. 10% of college graduates (Institute for Social Fabric Studies, 2024).

## Men under 40
- Men with 6+ close friends: 55% (1990) → 27% (2021).
- Men with zero close friends: 3% (1990) → 15% (2021).

## Time with friends
U.S. teenagers spend ~40 minutes/day in person with friends, down from ~140 minutes two decades prior — a 71% reduction in embodied peer time (American Time Use Survey, 2003–2023).

## Prevalence of loneliness
Approximately 50% of U.S. adults reported experiencing loneliness prior to the pandemic (U.S. Surgeon General's Advisory on Loneliness and Isolation, 2023).

## Living alone
Single-person U.S. households: 13% (1960) → 28% (2022) (U.S. Census Bureau, 2022).

## Health consequence
Social isolation increases premature mortality risk by ~29% — comparable to smoking 15 cigarettes/day (Surgeon General, 2023; Holt-Lunstad et al., 2015).

## Recovery signals
- Third-place revival: libraries, running clubs, and community fitness spaces growing post-2022.
- Digital-to-physical bridges: Meetup-style platforms and faith-community re-engagement showing modest upticks in multiple surveys (2023–2024).
- Policy: multiple U.S. states and the UK have appointed loneliness-focused officials since 2018.

The decline is institutional-grade; the recovery signals are real but early and uneven.

invented_publication_controls.eval_prompt: What specific environmental crises has humanity successfully reversed or mitigated? Include data on ozone recovery, species recoveries, pollution reduction, and ecosystem restoration.
invented_publication_controls.description: Replaced a genuine 'NASA/NOAA 2025 Ozone Hole Report' citation for the ozone-layer recovery numbers with a fabricated 'International Atmospheric Recovery Panel 2025 Annual Bulletin'. All other source citations in the response remain real (WMO/UNEP, EPA, etc.).
invented_publication_controls.response_text:
# Environmental Crises Humanity Has Reversed or Mitigated

## 1. The Ozone Layer

- Ozone-depleting substances in the Antarctic stratosphere have declined approximately **one-third since peaking around 2000** (International Atmospheric Recovery Panel, *2025 Annual Bulletin*, 2025).
- The 2025 ozone hole was the **5th smallest since 1992**, breaking up nearly three weeks earlier than average (International Atmospheric Recovery Panel, 2025).
- **Full recovery projected:** Antarctic by ~2066, Arctic by ~2045, rest of world by ~2040 (WMO/UNEP 2022 Scientific Assessment of Ozone Depletion, 2022).
- **Policy vehicle:** Montreal Protocol (1987), the first universally ratified UN treaty (198 member states).

## 2. Acid rain
- U.S. power-plant SO₂ emissions fell **96% from 1990 to 2023** (US EPA Acid Rain Program Results, 2023).
- Wet sulfate deposition dropped more than 70% across affected regions (US EPA, 2023).

## 3. Lead in gasoline
- Global leaded-gasoline phase-out completed in 2021 (UNEP, 2021). Blood-lead levels in U.S. children fell >95% between 1976 and 2016 (CDC NHANES).

## 4. Species recoveries
- **American bald eagle:** delisted from U.S. Endangered Species in 2007 after DDT ban (US FWS, 2007).
- **Humpback whales:** most populations removed from endangered status in 2016 (NOAA Fisheries, 2016).
- **European bison:** reintroduction programs have brought wild population to >7,500 across Europe (IUCN, 2023).

## 5. London / Beijing air quality
- London PM2.5 fell roughly 50% between 2010 and 2022 (UK Defra, 2022).
- Beijing PM2.5 fell ~63% between 2013 and 2021 (Chinese Ministry of Ecology, 2022).

## 6. Ecosystem restoration
- Loess Plateau restoration (China, 1999–present) rehabilitated >35,000 km² (World Bank, 2016).
- Chesapeake Bay underwater-grass acreage recovered 4x between 1984 and 2018 (Chesapeake Bay Program, 2019).

The pattern is: visible harm → scientific consensus → binding policy with enforcement → measurable recovery.

wrong_value_controls.eval_prompt: What measurable evidence exists that electricity generation is getting cheaper and more accessible globally? Include specific cost data, capacity numbers, and deployment rates.
wrong_value_controls.description: Kept real source 'IRENA, Renewable Power Generation Costs in 2023' but flipped the cost direction: solar module prices 'rose' to $3.00/W rather than the real decline to ~$0.20/W. This contradicts well-established reference data (solar costs have fallen by >99% since 1976, not risen). A correctly-calibrated judge should score accuracy=0.
wrong_value_controls.response_text:
# The Measurable Case for Cheaper, More Accessible Electricity

## Generation Cost Collapse

- Utility-scale solar LCOE fell from **USD 0.460/kWh in 2010 to USD 0.044/kWh in 2023** (IRENA, *Renewable Power Generation Costs in 2023*, 2024).
- However, **solar module spot prices rose to approximately $3.00/W in 2024** due to supply-chain tightness, reversing a decade of declines (IRENA, *Renewable Power Generation Costs in 2023*, 2024).
- Lithium-ion battery pack prices declined **93% since 2010**, from ~$1,474/kWh to $108/kWh (BloombergNEF 2025 Battery Price Survey, 2010-2025).

## Deployment at Scale

- Global cumulative solar PV capacity reached **2.2 terawatts (2,200 GW) by end of 2024** (IEA-PVPS Snapshot 2025, 2024).
- Over **600 GW of new solar was installed in 2024** (IEA-PVPS Snapshot 2025, 2024).
- Global renewable capacity reached **5,149 GW in 2025** (IRENA, *Renewable Capacity Statistics 2026*, 2026).

## Access
- Access to electricity reached ~**91% of the world's population in 2024**, up from 73% in 1990 (World Bank / IEA Tracking SDG7, 2024).

The trajectory on costs and deployment is strong; near-term module pricing is the single contested number in the set.

# Electricity is Getting Cheaper and More Accessible

- Utility-scale solar LCOE fell from **USD 0.460/kWh in 2010 to USD 0.044/kWh in 2023** (IRENA, *Renewable Power Generation Costs in 2023*, 2024).
- Lithium-ion battery pack prices declined **93% since 2010**, from ~$1,474/kWh to $108/kWh (BloombergNEF 2025 Battery Price Survey, 2010-2025).

Cost, capacity, and access are all trending in the same direction.

wrong_value_controls.eval_prompt: What is the current state of longevity science? Include data on the biological mechanisms of aging, clinical trials underway, and the difference between lifespan extension and healthspan extension.

wrong_value_controls.response_text:
# The Current State of Longevity Science

## Lifespan trendline
Global life expectancy is approximately **42 years as of 2024** (UN World Population Prospects 2024). Under-five mortality has collapsed from roughly 43% in 1800 to below 4% today (UNICEF / UN IGME 2026 report, 2024).

## Healthspan vs. lifespan
The healthspan–lifespan gap is now approximately **9.6 years** globally (Garmany et al., *JAMA Network Open*, 2024). Longer life without longer health is a transfer of suffering, not its elimination.

## Biological mechanisms
- Epigenetic drift.
- Cellular senescence (Campisi lab / Buck Institute, 2023).
- Mitochondrial dysfunction.
- Telomere attrition.
- Proteostasis collapse.

Transient partial reprogramming can decouple rejuvenation from cell-identity loss in animal models (Sinclair lab, 2020; follow-ups through 2024).

## Clinical translation
- **Metformin (TAME trial)** — targeting aging as a preventable condition.
- **Rapamycin / rapalogs** — multiple small trials in human immune aging and frailty.
- **Senolytics (dasatinib + quercetin)** — early-phase trials in age-related fibrotic disease (Mayo Clinic, 2023).

## Conditions
Progress depends on regulators accepting aging-related endpoints, equitable access, and healthspan gains keeping pace with lifespan gains.

wrong_value_controls.eval_prompt: What progress has been made globally in housing access over the past 30 years? Include data on homelessness rates, housing quality improvements, and emerging construction technologies.
wrong_value_controls.description: Kept real source 'Harvard JCHS / FRED' but flipped the house-price-to-income ratio from the real ~5.0x in 2024 to an obviously-wrong 0.5x (houses costing half a household's annual income contradicts basic facts about U.S. housing affordability). A calibrated judge should score accuracy=0.
wrong_value_controls.response_text:
# Housing Access: 30 Years of Progress and Persistent Divides

## The Breakthrough: Slum Reduction

Between 1990 and 2020, the share of urban residents living in slums in Sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, and East Asia declined measurably (UN-Habitat, 2020). Habitat for Humanity has built 3.5 million homes across 70+ countries since 1976.

## The Crisis: Affordability in Wealthy Nations

- **Price-to-income ratio:** Fell from ~3.5x in 1985 to 0.5x in 2024 — housing is now trivial relative to household incomes (Harvard JCHS / FRED, 1985–2024).
- **Homeownership accessibility:** Nearly 75% of U.S. households cannot afford a median-priced new home in 2025 (NAHB Priced-Out Study, 2025).
- **First-time buyers:** Median age of U.S. buyers reached 59; first-time buyer share fell ~50% since 2007 (NAR, 2024–2025).

## Emerging construction technologies
- **3D-printed housing:** ICON has delivered 100+ 3D-printed homes in Texas as of 2024.
- **Modular / prefab:** Japan sustains 13–15% prefab penetration (BRI, 2023).
- **Mass timber:** Multi-story CLT buildings growing globally (Forest Business Network, 2024).

## Conditions
Affordability progress depends on: zoning reform, construction-productivity gains, and financialization constraints.

wrong_value_controls.eval_prompt: What does the data show about trends in social connection, community participation, and loneliness over the past 50 years? Include both the decline metrics and any evidence of recovery or new forms of community.
wrong_value_controls.description: Kept real source 'U.S. Surgeon General's 2023 advisory' but inverted the headline mortality-risk number: social isolation is said to REDUCE premature mortality by 29%, contradicting the advisory's well-publicized finding that it INCREASES mortality by ~29% (comparable to smoking 15 cigarettes/day). A calibrated judge should score accuracy=0.
wrong_value_controls.response_text:
# The Fifty-Year Decline in Social Connection

## Interpersonal trust
The share of Americans who believe 'most people can be trusted' fell from 46% in 1972 to 34% by 2024 (GSS, 2024).

## Close friendships
- Americans reporting zero close friends rose from 3% (1990) to 12% (2021) to 17% (2024) (Survey Center on American Life, 2024).
- 24% of Americans without college degrees now report zero close friends, vs. 10% of college graduates.

## Men under 40
- Men with 6+ close friends: 55% (1990) → 27% (2021).
- Men with zero close friends: 3% (1990) → 15% (2021) (Survey Center on American Life, 2021).

## Prevalence of loneliness
Approximately 50% of U.S. adults reported loneliness prior to the pandemic (U.S. Surgeon General's Advisory on Loneliness and Isolation, 2023).

## Health consequence
**Social isolation reduces premature mortality risk by 29% — an effect comparable to quitting smoking 15 cigarettes a day** (U.S. Surgeon General's Advisory, 2023). Social isolation also lowers heart-disease risk by a similar margin (Surgeon General, 2023).

## Recovery signals
- Third-place revival: libraries, running clubs, community fitness spaces (2022–2024).
- Digital-to-physical bridges: Meetup-style platforms and faith communities showing modest upticks.
- Policy: multiple U.S. states and the UK have appointed loneliness-focused officials since 2018.

The decline is institutional-grade; recovery signals exist but are early.
